text.title: Agentic RAG Query Decomposition Core Rules
text.summary: 3 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every query-decomposer must enforce.

rule.statement: Decomposition produces 2–4 sub-queries. <2 → treat original as atomic (single-pass fallback); >4 → keep top-4 by planner confidence.
rule.rationale: <2 means atomic; >4 produces redundant overlapping retrievals inflating context.

rule.statement: Independent sub-queries dispatched in parallel (asyncio.gather or thread pool). Per-run cache by sub-query hash.
rule.rationale: Serial fanout adds one round-trip per sub-query unnecessarily.

rule.statement: Each sub-query MUST retrieve ≥1 high-confidence chunk (score > threshold). Zero-coverage sub-queries flagged for expansion, not silently dropped.
rule.rationale: Silent gaps produce answers that look complete but miss whole information domains.

rule.statement: Compound multi-intent query AND parallel retrieval available → wire decomposer.
Single-intent atomic query OR no parallel retriever → skip; use single-pass RAG.
